title: xylopia peekelii
intro: Xylopia peekelii, also known as the West African pepper or Guinea pepper, is a plant species belonging to the Annonaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Xylopia peekelii include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an anthelmintic to expel intestinal worms. Xylopia peekelii is typically prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, which can be used to make medicinal teas, tonics, and remedies.
